method: WebFetch triage + opendataloader-pdf extraction on the one downloadable PDF

tuscaloosa-city.pdf_title: Position Statement on AI in TCS
tuscaloosa-city.note: Full policy text recovered; 12 structured items, 2 headings, 10 paragraphs.

vestavia-hills.ai_attachment: Document M. Artificial Intelligence (AI) Use Guidelines Regarding Data Privacy
verbatim_excerpts: responsible use, management, and oversight of AI...data privacy, security, ethical use
applies to all AI systems, applications, and tools...including AI-driven analytics, automated decision-making tools, and AI-enhanced educational technologies
vestavia-hills.note: AI guidelines live in referenced Document M not inlined on policy index page; follow-up fetch needed to locate attachment.

athens-city.policy_container: Internet Safety and Acceptable Use Policy (Plagiarism section)
athens-city.verbatim_clause: Artificial intelligence is a vastly emerging technology...its use will be treated like any established technology resource in ACS. Any work produced must be properly cited and/or attributed to the generative AI engine. Any malicious or unethical use will be subject to the disciplinary actions outlined in the Code of Conduct.
athens-city.note: Short but genuine primary AI clause embedded in existing AUP; recovered verbatim.

2025-01-09 work session: draft AI policy presented
Aligns with ALSDE guidelines across 7 pillars: data governance, privacy/security, procurement, implementation, educator competency, risk management, utility/effectiveness
Next step: route to teacher/support-staff associations for feedback before formal board consideration
Educator training precedes student rollout
